info.title: TokenLab AI Gateway
info.description: Organization balance, API key management, and key-level usage/billing via management token

tags.name: Management
tags.description: Organization API key management and key-level usage/billing via management token

post.description: Creates a model response for the given chat conversation. Supports streaming and function calling.

schema.description: Server-Sent Events stream with JSON chunks

post.description: Creates an embedding vector representing the input text.

value.input: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog

post.description: Creates embeddings for multimodal input items. Text input is generally available; image input may require feature enablement.

input.text: Describe this product for semantic search.

post.description: Creates an image given a prompt.

value.prompt: A white cat sitting on a rainy windowsill
A cinematic product photo of matte black headphones on a glass desk

get.description: Compatibility alias for polling public async image task IDs such as `ldtask_...`. Prefer the `poll_url` returned by create responses, or use `/v1/tasks/{id}` as the canonical fixed status endpoint. This route uses the same authorization, ownership checks, billing headers, terminal snapshots, and response shape as the image-specific status handler.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The public async image task ID returned by `id` / `task_id`, or embedded in `poll_url`.

403.description: Task not available to the current API key

404.description: Task not found or no longer available

post.description: Generates audio from the input text (Text-to-Speech).

value.input: This is high quality speech synthesis.

post.description: Transcribes audio into text (Speech-to-Text).

Creates an asynchronous video generation task. The response returns a canonical task ID and usually a preferred `poll_url`; clients should poll `poll_url` first, or use `/v1/tasks/{id}` as the fixed status endpoint.

value.prompt: A cinematic shot of a golden retriever running through a field of sunflowers
Make this image come alive with gentle movement
Keep the same subject identity and palette while adding subtle motion

error.message: Seedance material is still preparing. Please retry after it becomes ACTIVE.

get.description: Retrieves the status and result of a video generation task. New clients should prefer the `poll_url` returned by create responses, or use `/v1/tasks/{id}` as the canonical fixed status endpoint. The `/v1/videos/generations/{id}` path remains for backward compatibility.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The async task ID returned by `id` / `task_id`, or embedded in `poll_url`

post.description: Uploads one Seedance material asset URL into a TokenLab material group. Real-person assets require the TokenLab group_id returned by the real-person verification bind flow.

get.description: Lists Seedance material assets owned by the authenticated organization. Use library_type, group_id, and status filters to find assets for a generation workflow.

parameters.name: group_id
parameters.description: Filter by TokenLab material asset group id.

post.description: Creates a TokenLab real-person material verification H5 session. The response contains only TokenLab session fields. The session and H5 link are valid for 120 seconds; the binding token is delivered through the TokenLab callback redirect after the H5 flow completes.

get.description: Lists TokenLab real-person material verification history for the authenticated organization. The response contains only TokenLab IDs and session status fields.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: Maximum number of history records to return.

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description: Pagination token from a previous response.

post.description: Binds a successful TokenLab real-person verification session to a TokenLab real-person material group. The request must include session_id and verification_token from the TokenLab callback redirect.

get.description: Retrieves one TokenLab Seedance material asset and its asynchronous ingestion status.

put.description: Updates a Seedance material asset owned by the authenticated organization.

delete.description: Deletes a Seedance material asset owned by the authenticated organization.

get.description: Retrieves the status and result of an asynchronous generation task. Prefer the `poll_url` returned by the create response; `/v1/tasks/{id}` is the canonical fixed status endpoint for video, music, and 3D jobs.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The async task ID returned by `id` / `task_id`, or embedded in `poll_url`

200.description: Task status (task-type specific fields may be present)

403.description: Task not available to the current API key

delete.description: Cancels a queued asynchronous task when cancellation is supported for the selected task. Cancellation currently supports queued Seedance video tasks (`seedance-1.5-pro`, `seedance-2.0`, and `seedance-2.0-fast`) while they are still waiting to run. Tasks that are already processing, completed, failed, expired, or unsupported are not cancelled.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The async task ID returned by `id` / `task_id`, or embedded in `poll_url`

200.description: Task cancelled. The task remains queryable and is represented as `failed` with `cancelled: true` for compatibility with existing async task status values.

400.description: Task type, provider, or model does not support cancellation

403.description: Task not available to the current API key

409.description: Task is no longer cancellable, for example because it is already processing or terminal

get.description: Lists the currently available models. Default responses are trimmed for discovery. Non-chat models may include lightweight request-format metadata; `agent_preferences` appears only when `recommended_for` is present. Deprecated `lemondata` is still returned as a compatibility alias during the migration.
Retrieves a model instance, providing basic information about the model.

post.description:
Creates a model response using Anthropic's native request format. Supports Claude models with vision and streaming.

**Authentication:** Use `x-api-key` header or `Authorization: Bearer` header.

schema.description: Anthropic-style SSE stream with message_start, content_block_delta, message_stop events

post.description: Counts input tokens for an Anthropic-compatible messages request without creating a model response. Authentication is required; the operation is not billed.
Creates a native Anthropic Message Batch. Requests use the same Anthropic message parameters as /v1/messages.

get.description: Lists native Anthropic Message Batches for the authenticated organization.

parameters.name: before_id
parameters.description: Return batches before this batch id

parameters.name: after_id
parameters.description: Return batches after this batch id

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of batches to return

get.description: Retrieves the status and metadata for a native Anthropic Message Batch.

delete.description: Deletes a native Anthropic Message Batch when the provider supports deletion.

get.description: Downloads JSONL results for a completed native Anthropic Message Batch.

200.description: Message batch results in JSONL format

post.description: Cancels a native Anthropic Message Batch when the provider still allows cancellation.

Generates content using Google Gemini API format. Supports text and vision models.

**Authentication:** Use `?key=` query parameter, `x-goog-api-key` header, or `Authorization: Bearer` header.

Streams content generation using Google Gemini API format. Returns Server-Sent Events.

**Authentication:** Use `?key=` query parameter, `x-goog-api-key` header, or `Authorization: Bearer` header.

schema.description: SSE stream with Gemini response chunks

post.description: Creates a response using the OpenAI Responses API format. Supports stateful conversation management.

model.description: Model to use

oneOf.description: Structured input items for the conversation.

input.description: Input content as a string or structured item array.

tools.description: Tools available to the model. For hosted image_generation tools that use the default image tool model or explicitly set model: gpt-image-2, TokenLab removes unsupported input_fidelity before forwarding because GPT Image 2 already treats image inputs as high fidelity. Do not send background: transparent for that tool; TokenLab does not silently remove it because that changes output semantics.

tool_choice.description: Tool choice policy or explicit tool selection.

include_obfuscation.description: When true, request obfuscated tokens in Responses stream events from compatible upstreams.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort hint for compatible models.

include.description: Additional response sections to include when supported.

service_tier.description: Service tier hint for compatible providers.

truncation_strategy.description: Truncation strategy for long conversations when supported.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether the model may issue parallel tool calls.

get.description: Retrieves a stored Responses API response by TokenLab response id. Ownership checks return 404 when the response is missing or unavailable to the current API key.

delete.description: Deletes a stored Responses API response by TokenLab response id. When applicable, TokenLab clears the provider-side record before clearing local state.

post.description: Compacts a Responses conversation for Codex CLI remote compaction. This endpoint uses `POST /v1/responses/compact`; do not use `/v1/compact`. The request `model` is the current Codex session model and must be available on the Responses path.

model.description: Model to use for compaction. Codex CLI sends the current session model.

stream.description: Streaming is not supported for compaction.

post.description: Reranks documents by relevance to a query using semantic similarity.

model.description: Reranker model to use

post.description: Creates a music generation task using Suno. Returns a task ID for polling.

prompt.description: Music description. Required for music, lyrics, upload-cover, and upload-extend requests; omit for add-instrumental.

negative_tags.description: Styles to avoid. Required when audio_operation is add-instrumental.

mv.description: Official Suno model version. Required when creating music (action omitted or MUSIC); omit for lyrics-only requests.

audio_url.description: Publicly reachable reference or uploaded audio URL. Defaults to upload-cover when audio_operation is omitted.

audio_operation.description: Uploaded-audio mode. upload-extend requires continue_at; add-instrumental requires audio_url, title, tags, and negative_tags.

continue_clip_id.description: Clip ID to continue when extending an existing generation.

continue_at.description: Timestamp in seconds for continuation flows. Required when audio_operation is upload-extend.

get.description: Retrieves the status and result of a music generation task.

post.description: Creates a 3D model generation task using Tripo3D. Returns a task ID for polling.

style.description: Style hint for compatible 3D model families.

get.description: Retrieves the status and result of a 3D generation task.
Returns the current organization balance totals for the authenticated management token.
Lists user-managed API keys for the current organization management token.

post.description: Creates a new user API key for the current organization management token.

name.description: Display name for the user API key. Trimmed server-side. Length must be 1-50 characters.

limitAmount.description: Spending limit input in USD. `null` or omitted means unlimited usage, and `0` means zero quota.

limitCurrency.description: Currency for `limitAmount`. USD is the only active currency. Sending `CNY` returns 400 `currency_retired`.

models.description: Optional model allowlist. Empty array means no per-key model restriction beyond organization policy.

expiresAt.description: Optional RFC3339 timestamp. null means no expiry.

patch.description: Updates a user-managed API key for the current organization management token.

status.description: Lifecycle status for the user API key.

name.description: Updated display name. Length must be 1-50 characters after trimming.

limitAmount.description: Updated spending limit input in USD. `null` means unlimited and `0` means zero quota.

limitCurrency.description: Currency for `limitAmount`. USD is the only active currency. Sending `CNY` returns 400 `currency_retired`.

expiresAt.description: Updated RFC3339 expiry timestamp. null clears the expiry.

schema.description: At least one field must be provided.

get.description: Returns paginated key-level usage details without exposing provider-specific execution details.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Default is 50 and the maximum is 100.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter by requested model name. Maximum length 100.

parameters.name: modelVendor
parameters.description: Filter by public model vendor. Maximum length 100.

parameters.name: accessChannel
parameters.description: Filter by access channel: platform billing or your own provider key (BYOK).

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: Inclusive date filter. Accepts either RFC3339 with timezone (for example 2026-03-28T00:00:00+08:00) or YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: Inclusive date filter. Accepts either RFC3339 with timezone (for example 2026-03-28T00:00:00+08:00) or YYYY-MM-DD.

get.description: Returns aggregated key-level billing breakdowns without exposing provider-specific execution details.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Filter by requested model name. Maximum length 100.

parameters.name: modelVendor
parameters.description: Filter by public model vendor. Maximum length 100.

parameters.name: accessChannel
parameters.description: Filter by access channel: platform billing or your own provider key (BYOK).

get.description: Retrieves pricing-only detail for one model. Use this endpoint for price explanation, not for non-chat request construction.

post.description: Uploads a JSONL batch input file or an image input file that can be referenced by file_id in supported image edit requests.

purpose.description: Use batch for Batch API JSONL files. Use user_data or vision for image files that will be referenced from image edits.

model.description: Optional TokenLab extension for image file uploads. Defaults to gpt-image-2 and binds the returned file_id to the selected image-edit configuration.

parameters.name: purpose
parameters.description: Optional file purpose filter. batch and batch_output cover Batch API files; user_data and vision cover TokenLab image/reference file bindings. Anthropic Files API mode is selected by Anthropic headers rather than this query parameter.

delete.description: Deletes a batch file or a bound image/reference file. When the request includes anthropic-beta: files-api-2025-04-14, the route uses Anthropic Files API mode for the bound workspace.

parameters.name: file_id
parameters.description: File ID returned by the Files API.

post.description: Creates an OpenAI-compatible batch job.

get.description: Returns the public pricing surface for active models, with optional provider and tag filters.

post.description: Translates text into a target language using the current translation request format.
Transcribes audio and translates the result to English.

get.description: Returns realtime endpoint metadata for normal HTTP GET requests. WebSocket clients use the same path with `?model={model}` and Bearer API key authentication to open realtime speech or multimodal sessions. This public surface is a TokenLab WebSocket proxy; it does not expose OpenAI Realtime REST helper endpoints such as client secrets, translation client secrets, Calls, or legacy beta session creation.

parameters.name: model
parameters.description: Realtime model ID to route the WebSocket session. Required for WebSocket upgrade requests; optional for plain HTTP metadata checks.

200.description: Realtime endpoint metadata returned for non-WebSocket GET requests

post.description: Edits an image using multipart image uploads, JSON image URLs, or the official JSON images array for supported GPT Image models.

model.description: Model to use for image edits. Send this explicitly.

image_url.description: Single source image URL. Kept for TokenLab compatibility; use images for the official JSON shape.

async.description: Return a task before the edited image is ready for models that support public async execution.

stream.description: Reserved image streaming flag. The public edit route currently rejects true.

image_urls.description: Multiple source image URLs. GPT Image edits accept up to 16 source images; xAI Grok Imagine edit models accept at most 3 source images.

images.description: Official JSON image references for image edits. Provide exactly one of image_url or file_id for each item. GPT Image edits accept up to 16 source images; xAI Grok Imagine edit models accept at most 3 source images.

file_id.description: A file id returned by TokenLab /v1/files and bound to the same image-edit configuration.

image_url.description: Source mask image URL. Public http/https URLs and compatible data URLs are supported by the runtime validation path.

file_id.description: A file id returned by TokenLab /v1/files and bound to the same image-edit configuration.

mask.description: Optional JSON mask reference for image edits. Provide exactly one of image_url or file_id. Multipart requests can also send mask as a file part.

image.description: Source image file(s). Repeat image for multiple source images. GPT Image edits accept up to 16 images; xAI Grok Imagine edit models accept at most 3 source images.

model.description: Model to use for image edits. Send this explicitly, for example gpt-image-2.

image[].description: Alternative repeated multipart field name for multiple source images. The same model-specific limits apply: GPT Image up to 16, xAI Grok Imagine up to 3.

post.description: Generates image variations from a multipart image upload.

model.description: Required model identifier for legacy variation-compatible routes. For current image-to-image workflows, prefer /v1/images/edits with gpt-image-2 or /v1/images/generations with an image-to-image model.

get.description: Lists enabled models through the Gemini-compatible v1beta model discovery surface.
Retrieves model metadata through the Gemini-compatible v1beta surface.

post.summary: Prepare World Labs media asset upload
post.description: Creates a World Labs media asset record and returns a signed upload URL. Use the returned media_asset_id from native world_prompt objects with source=media_asset. Media assets can only be used by the organization that created them.

extension.description: File extension without dot, such as jpg, png, or mp4.

get.description: Retrieves metadata for a World Labs media asset prepared through TokenLab. The media asset must belong to the authenticated organization.

post.description: Creates a World Labs Marble world generation task for TokenLab-supported Marble models. Returns a task ID for polling. TokenLab supports native world_prompt media_asset references when the media asset was prepared through TokenLab.

model.description: Marble model to use. Defaults to marble-1.0 to match the current World Labs compatibility default when model is omitted.

prompt.description: Text prompt used for text or guided image/video world generation.

display_name.description: Optional display name for the generated world, up to 64 characters.

world_prompt.description: Native World Labs world_prompt object for advanced callers. Supported prompt types are text, image, multi-image, and video. Content references may use source=media_asset with a media_asset_id prepared by /v1/worlds/media-assets/prepare-upload.

image.description: Base64 or data URL image prompt.

images.description: Shortcut multi-image prompts. Provide up to 4 images. Use native world_prompt.reconstruct_images=true for up to 8 images.

is_pano.description: Whether an image prompt is an existing panorama. Use true for pano input, false for single-image input, or auto.

tags.description: Optional World Labs tags. Provide up to 10 tags, each up to 32 characters.

get.description: Retrieves the status and result of a World Labs Marble world generation task.

post.description: Lists World Labs worlds created through TokenLab for the authenticated organization. Results only include resources created through TokenLab.

sort_order.description: TokenLab extension for ascending or descending order.

get.description: Retrieves a World Labs world generated through TokenLab. The world ID must be owned by the authenticated organization.

delete.description: Deletes a World Labs world generated through TokenLab. This permanently deletes the world and associated assets when World Labs accepts the deletion. TokenLab keeps task status records for usage history.

post.description: Creates a TokenLab Seedance virtual-avatar material group. Real-person material groups are created by the real-person verification bind flow.

get.description: Lists Seedance material groups owned by the authenticated organization. Real-person groups are returned after a successful verification bind flow.

parameters.name: library_type
parameters.description: Filter by TokenLab material library class.

get.description: Retrieves one TokenLab Seedance material group.

put.description: Updates display metadata for a TokenLab Seedance material group.

delete.description: Deletes a TokenLab Seedance material group and its material assets. The operation is irreversible when accepted.

post.description: Creates a Seedance 2.0 video generation task using a Volc-style REST body. Authentication uses a TokenLab Bearer API key.

get.description: Lists recent Seedance compatibility tasks for the authenticated organization.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Compatibility task ID returned by the create endpoint. IDs start with cgt-.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Compatibility task ID returned by the create endpoint. IDs start with cgt-.

200.description: Cancellation accepted. The response body is empty.

get.description: Dispatches Volc-style Action requests. Use Version=2024-01-01 or omit Version.

200.description: Action response. Create/Get/List actions return JSON; DeleteContentsGenerationsTasks returns an empty body.

post.description: Dispatches Volc-style Action requests. The Action and Version fields may be in the query string or JSON body.

200.description: Action response. Create/Get/List actions return JSON; DeleteContentsGenerationsTasks returns an empty body.

BearerAuth.description: API Key authentication. Get your API key from the dashboard.

ManagementTokenAuth.description: Management token authentication. Get your management token from Dashboard Settings.

model.description: ID of the model to use (e.g., gpt-5.4, claude-sonnet-4-6)

messages.description: A list of messages comprising the conversation

stream.description: If true, returns a stream of events

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

max_completion_tokens.description: Maximum completion tokens for newer reasoning-enabled model families

tools.description: List of tools the model may call

stream_options.description: Streaming options such as usage chunk inclusion.

parallel_tool_calls.description: Whether the model may issue parallel tool calls.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning effort hint for compatible model families.

logprobs.description: Whether to return log probabilities for output tokens.

top_logprobs.description: Number of most likely tokens to return at each position when logprobs is enabled.

top_k.description: Top-k sampling cutoff for compatible providers.

modalities.description: Requested output modalities such as text or audio.

audio.description: Audio output configuration when requesting audio modality.

prediction.description: Prediction hints for providers that support draft or speculative decoding.

service_tier.description: Service tier hint for compatible providers.

role.description: The role of the message author

name.description: Name of the function/tool (for function/tool messages)

tool_calls.description: Tool calls made by the assistant

tool_call_id.description: ID of the tool call being responded to

model.description: Model used for completion
ID of the model to use

input.description: Input text(s) or token array(s) to embed.

model.description: Model to use. Send this explicitly; query GET /v1/models?recommended_for=image for current recommendations.

operation.description: Public image operation family. Reference-image models use image-to-image with image_url, image_urls, or reference_image_urls.

image_url.description: Single reference image URL for compatible image-to-image models.

image_urls.description: Reference image URLs for compatible image-to-image models.

reference_image_urls.description: Alias used by compatible reference-image model families.

mask_url.description: Optional mask URL for compatible image operations.

size.description: Image size. For gpt-image-2, use auto or WIDTHxHEIGHT; custom dimensions must both be multiples of 16, longest edge <= 3840px, long/short ratio <= 3:1, and total pixels between 655,360 and 8,294,400.

quality.description: Image quality. GPT Image models such as gpt-image-2 use auto/low/medium/high. Other image families may use provider-specific values.

style.description: Optional model-specific style selector. Only send when the selected model documents support for this parameter.

expand_prompt.description: Ask compatible models to expand or enhance the prompt.

negative_prompt.description: Content to avoid for compatible image models.

async.description: Return a task before the final image is ready when the selected model supports public async execution.

background.description: Background handling for compatible image generation flows such as gpt-image-2 text-to-image. Not supported for gpt-image-2 image edits.

output_format.description: Output image format for compatible image models such as gpt-image-2

output_compression.description: Output compression level from 0 to 100 for compressed formats

compression.description: Alias for output_compression when supported by the selected model

moderation.description: Moderation strictness for compatible image models such as gpt-image-2

partial_images.description: Number of partial image events requested from compatible streaming-capable models.

input_fidelity.description: Input fidelity hint for compatible reference-image models.

stream.description: Reserved image streaming flag. The public generation route currently rejects true.

aspect_ratio.description: Aspect-ratio selector for compatible image model families.

resolution.description: Resolution selector for compatible image model families.

model.description: Model to use. Send this explicitly.

image.description: Local reference image upload for compatible image-to-image models.

reference_image_urls.description: Comma-separated reference image URLs for compatible model families.

revised_prompt.description: Revised prompt returned by models that rewrite or enhance the submitted prompt.

model.description: TTS model. Query GET /v1/models?recommended_for=tts for the current shortlist.

voice.description: Voice selector for OpenAI-compatible, Gemini, xAI, and MiniMax-compatible routes. Some MiniMax routes also accept voice_id.

voice_id.description: Provider-native voice selector for MiniMax-compatible speech models.

instructions.description: Optional style or delivery instructions for OpenAI-compatible TTS models that support them.

prompt.description: Optional speaking style prompt for Gemini TTS models.

language_code.description: Optional language code for Gemini, xAI, and compatible TTS routes.

response_format.description: Audio format. Common values include mp3, opus, aac, flac, wav, and pcm. Supported values vary by model family.

stream_format.description: TokenLab delivery format. stream_format=sse is not supported for tts-1 or tts-1-hd.

speed.description: Speech speed for model families that support it.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature for Gemini-compatible TTS routes.

model.description: Model to use (whisper-1)

timestamp_granularities.description: Timestamp granularity selection. Requires verbose_json output when requesting word-level timestamps.

model.description: Model to use (e.g., veo3.1, kling-v1)

operation.description: Requested video operation. If omitted, TokenLab infers the operation from the provided inputs. Explicit operation is recommended for production reliability.

image.description: Inline image as a data URL (for example, data:image/png;base64,...). Prefer image_url for broader production compatibility. When the selected Seedance model can use the TokenLab material library, TokenLab prepares this image input as a reusable material before generation; if it is not ACTIVE within 60 seconds, the API returns 409 seedance_material_preparing with auto_material_asset_ids. If the selected model cannot use the material library, ordinary image inputs continue on the regular image path.

image_url.description: Publicly reachable image URL for image-to-video generation. Preferred over inline base64 in production. When the selected Seedance model can use the TokenLab material library, TokenLab prepares this image input as a reusable material before generation; if it is not ACTIVE within 60 seconds, the API returns 409 seedance_material_preparing with auto_material_asset_ids. If the selected model cannot use the material library, ordinary image inputs continue on the regular image path.

video_url.description: Publicly reachable video URL for video-to-video style flows and motion-control models.

duration.description: Video duration in seconds. Seedance models also accept -1 to let the model choose within its supported duration range; TokenLab estimates billing conservatively for that mode.

aspect_ratio.description: Canonical video aspect ratio. Seedance defaults to adaptive when omitted. The ratio alias is accepted for compatibility.

resolution.description: Video resolution. Seedance defaults to 720p when omitted; available values are model-dependent.

output_audio.description: Canonical model-dependent audio output toggle. Veo 3 and Seedance requests default to true when omitted. Kling 3.0 Video accepts this selector for non-element-reference requests and maps it to the compatible sound control; omitted Kling requests default to silent output. Do not combine output_audio=true with kling_elements. The aliases outputAudio and generate_audio are accepted for compatibility and must match when provided together.

negative_prompt.description: What to avoid in the video

seed.description: Seed for reproducibility. Seedance uses -1 for random seed when omitted.

cfg_scale.description: Prompt adherence strength (0-20) for models that expose CFG-style control.

motion_strength.description: Motion intensity (0-1) for models that expose it.

start_image.description: First frame image input for start-end-to-video flows. When the selected Seedance model can use the TokenLab material library, TokenLab prepares this image input as a reusable material before generation; if it is not ACTIVE within 60 seconds, the API returns 409 seedance_material_preparing with auto_material_asset_ids. If the selected model cannot use the material library, ordinary image inputs continue on the regular image path.

end_image.description: Last frame image input for start-end-to-video flows. When the selected Seedance model can use the TokenLab material library, TokenLab prepares this image input as a reusable material before generation; if it is not ACTIVE within 60 seconds, the API returns 409 seedance_material_preparing with auto_material_asset_ids. If the selected model cannot use the material library, ordinary image inputs continue on the regular image path.

image_urls.description: Image URL array for compatible image-conditioned video flows. This endpoint currently allows up to 9 entries. When the selected Seedance model can use the TokenLab material library, TokenLab prepares this image input as reusable material before generation; if it is not ACTIVE within 60 seconds, the API returns 409 seedance_material_preparing with auto_material_asset_ids. If the selected model cannot use the material library, ordinary image inputs continue on the regular image path.

kling_elements.description: Kling 3.0 element references for kling-3.0-video image-conditioned requests. Define 1-3 elements and reference them in the prompt with @name. Each element requires 2-4 image URLs in element_input_urls. Do not combine kling_elements with output_audio=true; omit output_audio or set it to false for element-reference requests.

name.description: Element name used in the prompt without the @ prefix.

description.description: Optional short description of the element.

element_input_urls.description: Reference image URLs for this element. Kling 3.0 expects 2-4 images per element.

reference_images.description: Canonical public reference-image field for reference-to-video conditioning. This endpoint currently allows up to 9 URLs or compatible data URLs; model-specific limits can be lower. xAI grok-imagine-video accepts up to 7 image references with duration capped at 10 seconds, and grok-imagine-video-1.5-preview does not accept reference images. When the selected Seedance model can use the TokenLab material library, TokenLab prepares this image input as reusable material before generation; if it is not ACTIVE within 60 seconds, the API returns 409 seedance_material_preparing with auto_material_asset_ids. If the selected model cannot use the material library, ordinary image inputs continue on the regular image path.

material_asset_id.description: TokenLab Seedance material asset ID returned by /v1/videos/assets, by automatic image preparation, or by a real-person verification bind flow. Use it after the asset is ACTIVE with Seedance models that can use the TokenLab material library. The asset must belong to the current account.

material_asset_ids.description: TokenLab Seedance material asset IDs returned by /v1/videos/assets or by automatic image preparation. They share the Seedance image-reference limit; the selected model must be able to use the TokenLab material library and all assets must belong to the current account and be ACTIVE.

reference_image_type.description: Optional reference image role for models that distinguish between asset and style references. The camelCase alias referenceImageType is accepted for compatibility.

audio_url.description: Publicly reachable audio URL for model-specific audio-conditioned video flows.

task_id.description: Task identifier used by some continuation, extension, or derivative flows.

extend_at.description: Model-specific extension start offset used by some video-extension flows.

extend_times.description: Model-specific extension multiplier or repeat count used by some video-extension flows.

seconds.description: Alias of duration used by compatible request shapes. Seedance models also accept -1 to let the model choose within its supported duration range; duration and seconds must match when both are provided.

size.description: Model-specific size tier for compatible video models.

watermark.description: Optional watermark toggle for models that expose it. Seedance defaults to false when omitted.

effect_type.description: Model-specific effect selector for specialized editing flows.

video_urls.description: Compatibility array for video-conditioned flows when multiple input videos are supported by the routed model family. TokenLab currently accepts up to 3 reference videos.

audio_urls.description: Compatibility array for audio-conditioned flows when multiple audio references are supported by the routed model family. TokenLab currently accepts up to 3 reference audios.

ratio.description: Compatibility alias for aspect_ratio. If both ratio and aspect_ratio are provided, they must match.

outputAudio.description: Compatibility alias for output_audio. If both are provided, they must match.

generate_audio.description: Compatibility alias for output_audio. If more than one audio toggle is provided, all values must match.

execution_expires_after.description: Optional execution expiry window in seconds for compatible video models. Seedance defaults to 172800 seconds when omitted.

priority.description: Optional task priority for compatible video models. Do not combine priority with service_tier=flex.

safety_identifier.description: Optional safety/user trace identifier for compatible video models. If omitted for Seedance, TokenLab uses user when provided.

service_tier.description: Optional service tier for compatible video models. service_tier=default is accepted as a no-op for Seedance 2.0 models; service_tier=flex is rejected where the selected model does not support it.

frames.description: Optional frame count for compatible video models. Seedance 2.0 models and Seedance 1.5 Pro do not support this field.

camera_fixed.description: Optional fixed-camera selector for compatible video models. Seedance 2.0 models do not support this field.

return_last_frame.description: Return the last generated frame when the selected model supports it. Seedance defaults to false when omitted.

draft.description: Seedance 1.5 Pro draft workflow flag. Only supported by draft-capable Seedance routes; draft=true creates a low-cost draft task.

draft_task_id.description: Seedance 1.5 Pro draft promotion task ID. Provide this instead of draft=true to create the final video from a previous draft task.

task_id.description: Canonical async task identifier for polling. Treat this as the same identity as id when both are present.

poll_url.description: Preferred polling URL for this task. Use this exact path when checking status.

video_url.description: Generated video URL when the create response is already terminal or includes an immediate result.

last_frame_url.description: Last generated frame URL when requested and supported by the selected model.

generate_audio.description: Resolved audio output flag when returned by the adapter.

draft.description: Whether the Seedance draft workflow was used for this task.

draft_task_id.description: Seedance draft task identifier when returned by the adapter.

progress.description: Optional progress value returned only when a real progress value is available; use status to determine completion.

cancelled.description: True when a queued async task was cancelled before execution. Cancelled tasks are represented with `status: failed` for compatibility with existing async task status values.

cancellation_status.description: Cancellation marker for tasks cancelled through the public async task endpoint.

asset_url.description: Publicly reachable source URL to import. TokenLab stores the material for later use; do not rely on this URL as the long-term material address.

asset_name.description: Optional display name stored with the material asset.

library_type.description: Material library class. aigc_avatar is for virtual-avatar materials. liveness_face is for verified real-person materials.

group_id.description: TokenLab material group ID. Required for liveness_face materials; optional for aigc_avatar.

metadata.description: Optional client metadata stored with the material asset.

id.description: TokenLab material asset ID. Use this as material_asset_id after the asset becomes ACTIVE.

group_id.description: TokenLab material group ID that owns this material asset.

ready.description: True when the asset is active and not deleted.

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the material asset record was created.

lemondata.description: Deprecated alias for tokenlab. Retained for compatibility during the TokenLab migration.

model.description: Model associated with the error
Model to use (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-6)

max_tokens.description: Maximum number of tokens to generate

tools.description: Tool definitions available to the model.

tool_choice.description: Tool choice policy or explicit tool selection.

metadata.description: Request metadata echoed into downstream logs or traces when supported.

thinking.description: Thinking configuration for compatible Anthropic-style models.

stream_options.description: Streaming options such as usage chunk inclusion.

service_tier.description: Service tier hint for compatible providers.

candidateCount.description: Number of response candidates for non-streaming generation. Streaming requests must omit this field or keep it at 1.

responseModalities.description: Requested output modalities for compatible native Gemini routes.

responseMimeType.description: Requested output MIME type, such as text/plain or application/json.

responseSchema.description: JSON schema for structured output when responseMimeType requests JSON.

thinkingConfig.description: Thinking budget options for compatible Gemini models.

thinking_config.description: Snake-case thinking budget options for compatible Gemini models.

tools.description: Gemini tools. Supported tool combinations depend on the selected model and route; unsupported native image-output tool combinations are rejected before upstream retries.

toolConfig.description: Gemini tool configuration such as functionCallingConfig.

cachedContent.description: Gemini cached content resource name for compatible models.

organization_id.description: Organization associated with the current management token.

balance.description: Current organization balance in USD as a JSON number for display.

total_recharge.description: Total successful organization recharge amount in USD as a JSON number for display.

total_used.description: Total organization usage spend in USD as a JSON number for display.

balance_decimal.description: Exact current organization balance in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

total_recharge_decimal.description: Exact total successful organization recharge amount in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

total_used_decimal.description: Exact total organization usage spend in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

status.description: Lifecycle status for the user API key: active, inactive, or revoked.

limit_amount.description: API key spending cap in USD as a JSON number for display. null means unlimited.

used_amount.description: Accumulated API key usage in USD as a JSON number for display.

limit_amount_decimal.description: Exact API key spending cap in USD as a decimal string. null means unlimited.

used_amount_decimal.description: Exact accumulated API key usage in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

billing_transaction_id.description: Settled billing transaction id for request-level reconciliation.

cost.description: Request cost in USD as a JSON number for display.

usage_quantity_decimal.description: Exact usage quantity as a decimal string for non-token units.

cost_decimal.description: Exact request cost in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

total_cost.description: Aggregated total cost in USD as a JSON number for display.

total_cost_decimal.description: Exact aggregated total cost in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

cost.description: Cost in USD as a JSON number for display.
Cost in USD as a JSON number for display.

cost_decimal.description: Exact cost in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

cost.description: Cost in USD as a JSON number for display.
Cost in USD as a JSON number for display.

cost_decimal.description: Exact cost in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

ModelListLemondataExtension.description: TokenLab discovery fields for the list route. Detail-only metadata such as capability flags, pricing provenance, full model details, and empty recommendation snapshots are omitted.

lemondata.description: Deprecated alias for tokenlab. Retained for compatibility during the TokenLab migration.

ModelLemondataExtension.description: TokenLab extension fields for the detail route, including pricing, capabilities, recommendation metadata, and detailed non-chat request metadata.

purpose.description: File purpose. batch and batch_output are used by Batches; user_data and vision are used by file-backed image/reference requests.

status.description: Batch file processing status. Image/reference file objects may omit this field.

FileObject.description: File metadata. Batch files include status; image/reference files may omit status depending on the upstream file provider.

model.description: Model to count tokens for

cache_control.description: Top-level Anthropic cache control marker for count_tokens requests.

output_config.description: Anthropic output configuration fields that may affect token counting.

thinking.description: Anthropic extended thinking configuration for token counting.

tools.description: Tool definitions available to the model.

tool_choice.description: Tool choice policy or explicit tool selection.

input_tokens.description: Number of input tokens in the supplied Anthropic messages request

custom_id.description: Client-supplied identifier for this batch item

AnthropicMessageBatch.description: Anthropic-compatible message batch object relayed from the provider.

processing_status.description: Current processing status for the batch

request_counts.description: Upstream request count summary for the batch

ended_at.description: ISO timestamp when processing ended, when available

created_at.description: ISO timestamp when the batch was created, when available

expires_at.description: ISO timestamp when the batch expires, when available

model.description: Model to use for multimodal embeddings

dimensions.description: Optional embedding dimensionality when supported by the selected model

MultimodalEmbeddingInputItem.description: A multimodal embedding input item. Text input is generally available; image input may require feature enablement.

ModelListTokenLabExtension.description: TokenLab discovery fields for the list route. Detail-only metadata such as capability flags, pricing provenance, full model details, and empty recommendation snapshots are omitted.

stage.description: Provider lifecycle stage for public discovery and display.

reason.description: Short lifecycle reason or provider note.

retired_at.description: Official retirement date when the model is no longer public-discoverable.

sunset_at.description: Future sunset date for deprecated models.

latest.description: Whether the model should receive a latest badge.

latest_source.description: How the latest badge was derived.

latest_window_days.description: Window used for automatic latest badges.

user_charge_policy.description: User billing policy for this model.

free_reason.description: Reason a model is free to users, if applicable.

free_until.description: End time for a temporary free policy, if applicable.

badges.description: Display badges derived from lifecycle and commercial metadata.

ModelTokenLabExtension.description: TokenLab extension fields for the detail route, including pricing, capabilities, recommendation metadata, and detailed non-chat request metadata.

stage.description: Provider lifecycle stage for public discovery and display.

reason.description: Short lifecycle reason or provider note.

retired_at.description: Official retirement date when the model is no longer public-discoverable.

sunset_at.description: Future sunset date for deprecated models.

latest.description: Whether the model should receive a latest badge.

latest_source.description: How the latest badge was derived.

latest_window_days.description: Window used for automatic latest badges.

user_charge_policy.description: User billing policy for this model.

free_reason.description: Reason a model is free to users, if applicable.

free_until.description: End time for a temporary free policy, if applicable.

badges.description: Display badges derived from lifecycle and commercial metadata.

group_name.description: Optional display name for the material group.

library_type.description: Material library class. Direct creation supports aigc_avatar. Create liveness_face groups through real-person material verification.

id.description: TokenLab material group ID. Use it as group_id when uploading materials.

asset_url.description: Replacement publicly reachable material asset URL.

asset_name.description: Updated display name stored with the material asset.

metadata.description: Updated client metadata stored with the material asset.

cost.description: Cost in USD as a JSON number for display.

cost_decimal.description: Exact cost in USD as a decimal string for reconciliation.

callback_url.description: Public HTTPS URL that TokenLab redirects to after the H5 verification flow. TokenLab appends session_id, verification_token, verification_status, and result_code.

language.description: Language parameter appended to the returned H5 link.

h5_link.description: Short-lived H5 verification link for the end user. Valid for 120 seconds.

callback_url.description: Customer callback URL originally supplied in the request.

expires_in.description: Validity window in seconds for the session and H5 link.

verification_token.description: TokenLab opaque token from the TokenLab callback redirect.

group_name.description: Optional TokenLab display name for the bound real-person material group.

group_id.description: TokenLab material group ID to pass as group_id when creating real-person material assets.

callback_url.description: Customer callback URL originally supplied when the session was created.

status.description: TokenLab history status. A created or callback_succeeded session is reported as expired after the 120-second session window if it was not bound.

result_code.description: Neutral result code forwarded by TokenLab callback, when available.

verification_status.description: Neutral callback status, when TokenLab has received the H5 callback.

group_id.description: TokenLab material group ID after the verification result has been bound.

group_name.description: TokenLab material group display name after binding, when available.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds for the 120-second binding window.

callback_received_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when TokenLab received the callback.

bound_at.description: Unix timestamp in milliseconds when TokenLab bound the material group.

key.description: Full secret API key value returned only on creation. Store it immediately.

VolcSeedanceContentItem.description: Volc-style content item. Use text, image_url, video_url, audio_url, or draft_task. For image_url, omitting role treats the image as first_frame; use role=reference_image for reference images.

text.description: Text prompt content. Required when type=text.

role.description: Media role. first_frame/last_frame inputs cannot be mixed with reference_image/reference_video/reference_audio inputs.

image_url.description: Image URL. The object form {"url":"https://..."} matches the official request shape; a plain string is accepted for convenience.

draft_task.description: Draft task reference. The object form {"id":"cgt-..."} matches the official request shape.

model.description: Seedance model or endpoint identifier. TokenLab maps supported Seedance 2.0 identifiers to the matching platform model.

ratio.description: Output aspect ratio. Defaults to adaptive when omitted.

duration.description: Video duration in seconds. Seedance 2.0 supports normal integer durations and -1 for automatic duration where the selected model supports it.

resolution.description: Requested output resolution. 4k is only available on supported Seedance 2.0 routes; fast/mini availability depends on model capability.

generate_audio.description: Whether to generate audio. Defaults to true for Seedance 2.0 compatible requests.

return_last_frame.description: Return the final frame URL when supported.

seed.description: Random seed. -1 lets the model choose a seed. Forwarded on a best-effort basis for routes that accept it.

execution_expires_after.description: Task execution expiration window in seconds.

service_tier.description: default is accepted. flex is rejected for Seedance 2.0 compatibility tasks because the selected model family does not support it here.

tools.description: Optional tools such as {"type":"web_search"} when supported by the selected model.

draft.description: draft=true is not supported for Seedance 2.0 compatibility tasks and is rejected.

frames.description: Not supported for Seedance 2.0 compatibility tasks. Use duration instead.

camera_fixed.description: camera_fixed=true is not supported for Seedance 2.0 compatibility tasks. false is accepted as a no-op.

callback_url.description: Not supported in this compatibility endpoint. Poll task status instead.

progress.description: Optional progress value returned only when a real progress value is available; use status to determine completion.

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - Invalid or missing API key

PaymentRequired.description: Payment Required - Insufficient balance or quota exceeded

error.message: Insufficient balance. Please top up your account.
Rate limit exceeded. Please slow down.
All available channels failed to process the request
